devanagari: शौनक उवाच । कपिलस्तत्त्वसङ्ख्याता भगवान् आत्ममायया । जातः स्वयमजः साक्षाद् आत्मप्रज्ञप्तये नृणाम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śaunaka uvāca kapilastattvasaṅkhyātā bhagavān ātmamāyayā jātaḥ svayamajaḥ sākṣād ātmaprajñaptaye nṛṇām

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation:
Śaunaka said:
Kapila, the expounder of the Tattvas[1] (fundamental principles) i.e. of Sāṅkhya Śāstra is himself devoid of birth.[2] But for the exposition of the true nature of the Self (ātman) to men, he directly manifested himself of his own accord through his own Māyā.[3]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
tattva-saṅkhyāta—(i) Expounder of the correct knowledge of avyakta and other tattvas—Padaratnāvalī
(ii) Exponent of the Sāṅkhya system of philosophy.—VC., Bālaprabodhini

Footnote [2]:
ajaḥ [aja]—(i) Devoid of birth as a result of previous karmas—Bhāgavata Candrikā, Bālaprabodhini
(ii) Śrī Nārāyaṇa himself and not an āveśa—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) Whose līlā of taking birth is superhuman—not the ordinary one—VC.

Footnote [3]:
ātma-māyā [māyayā]—(i) Out of his compassion for devotees—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa
(ii) Of his own free will—Padaratnāvalī, Bālaprabodhini
(iii) By his incomprehensible power called Māyā—VC.
(iv) By his power of creating everything—Subodhinī

devanagari: न ह्यस्य वर्ष्मणः पुंसां वरिम्णः सर्वयोगिनाम् । विश्रुतौ श्रुतदेवस्य भूरि तृप्यन्ति मेऽसवः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyasya varṣmaṇaḥ puṃsāṃ varimṇaḥ sarvayoginām viśrutau śrutadevasya bhūri tṛpyanti me

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ tṛp class 4 verb]

translation: I have heard many times about the Lord[4] (Kapila). But really my senses are not fully satisfied by hearing his glory. Kapila is the senior-most (best) among all persons and the greatest among the Yogins.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Śruta-devasya—also is taken to qualify Kapila and means ‘Whose glory is described in the Vedas’—Subodhinī
‘Who becomes manifest in the heart of the audience by listening to Śāstras’—Siddhāntapradīpa

devanagari: यद् यद् विधत्ते भगवान् स्वच्छन्दात्मात्ममायया । तानि मे श्रद्दधानस्य कीर्तन्यान्यनुकीर्तय ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yad yad vidhatte bhagavān svacchandātmātmamāyayā tāni me śraddadhānasya kīrtanyānyanukīrtaya

translation: I am full of faith (in him). Narrate to me the praiseworthy deeds of the Lord who, though of absolutely in dependent mind, assumes body through his own Māyā power for (the fulfilment of) the wishes of his devotees.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । द्वैपायनसखस्त्वेवं मैत्रेयो भगवांस्तथा । प्राहेदं विदुरं प्रीत आन्वीक्षिक्यां प्रचोदितः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca dvaipāyanasakhastvevaṃ maitreyo bhagavāṃstathā prāhedaṃ viduraṃ prīta ānvīkṣikyāṃ pracoditaḥ

translation:
Sūta said:
(Just as you now ask me), venerable Maitreya, the friend of Dvaipāyana was also urged (by Vidura) to explain the Science of Soul. Being pleased, he expounded this (doctrine) to Vidura.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । पितरि प्रस्थितेऽरण्यं मातुः प्रियचिकीर्षया । तस्मिन् बिन्दुसरेऽवात्सीत् भगवान् कपिलः किल ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca pitari prasthite tasmin bindusare

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
When his father (Kardama) left for the forest, (they say that) praiseworthy Lord Kapila stayed in the Bindusaras with a desire to do good to his mother.

devanagari: तमासीनमकर्माणं तत्त्वमार्गाग्रदर्शनम् । स्वसुतं देवहूत्याह धातुः संस्मरती वचः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tamāsīnamakarmāṇaṃ tattvamārgāgradarśanam svasutaṃ devahūtyāha dhātuḥ saṃsmaratī vacaḥ
translation: Devahūti who remembered the words of the creator (Brahmā), spoke the following words to her son who was sitting quietly (actionless), and who guided through, to the end of the Path of Knowledge (or Sāṅkhya Śāstra).

devanagari: देवहूतिरुवाच । निर्विण्णा नितरां भूमन् असत् इन्द्रियतर्षणात् । येन सम्भाव्यमानेन प्रपन्नान्धं तमः प्रभो ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: devahūtiruvāca nirviṇṇā nitarāṃ bhūman asat indriyatarṣaṇāt yena sambhāvyamānena prapannāndhaṃ tamaḥ prabho

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Devahūti said:
Oh All-pervading Lord, I am extremely disgusted with the thirst for enjoying the objects of wicked senses. By catering to the (urge of the) senses, I find myself lost in the darkness of ignorance.

devanagari: तस्य त्वं तमसोऽन्धस्य दुष्पारस्याद्य पारगम् । सच्चक्षुर्जन्मनामन्ते लब्धं मे त्वदनुग्रहात् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya tvaṃ tamaso saccakṣurjanmanāmante labdhaṃ me tvadanugrahāt

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ labh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ labh class 1 verb]

translation: Today, at the end of many births, and by your grace, I have secured you as a good vision (insight) which leads to the end of this blinding darkness of ignorance which is very difficult to cross.

devanagari: य आद्यो भगवान् पुंसां ईश्वरो वै भवान्किल । लोकस्य तमसान्धस्य चक्षुः सूर्य इवोदितः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ya ādyo bhagavān puṃsāṃ īśvaro vai bhavānkila lokasya tamasāndhasya cakṣuḥ sūrya ivoditaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: Your Honour is verily the first glorious Lord, the controller of all human beings. Just like the rising Sun (enabling people to see) you are an eye to the world which is blinded with the darkness of ignorance.

devanagari: अथ मे देव सम्मोहं अपाक्रष्टुं त्वमर्हसि । योऽवग्रहोऽहं मम इति इति एतस्मिन् योजितस्त्वया ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: atha me deva sammohaṃ apākraṣṭuṃ tvamarhasi yo
translation: Hence, Oh Lord, you should dispel my delusion—the delusion viz. strong attachment to my body and my belongings (as ‘me and mine’) which has been created by you (in us).

devanagari: तं त्वा गताहं शरणं शरण्यं । स्वभृत्यसंसारतरोः कुठारम् । जिज्ञासयाहं प्रकृतेः पूरुषस्य । नमामि सद्धर्मविदां वरिष्ठम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tvā gatāhaṃ śaraṇaṃ śaraṇyaṃ svabhṛtyasaṃsārataroḥ kuṭhāram jijñāsayāhaṃ prakṛteḥ pūruṣasya namāmi saddharmavidāṃ variṣṭham
translation: With a desire to know (the real nature) of Prakṛti and Puruṣa, I have come for shelter to you who are worth resorting to for protection. I bow to you who are like an axe (to cut down and put an end) to the tree of Saṃsāra of your devotees and are the greatest among the knowers of true dharma.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
saddharma: (i) nivṛtti dharma—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Śama and other brahma-dharmas—Subodhinī
(iii) The way to Mokṣa—Bālaprabodhini
(iv) Path-of Bhakti—VC.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति स्वमातुर्निरवद्यमीप्सितं । निशम्य पुंसां अपवर्गवर्धनम् । धियाभिनन्द्यात्मवतां सतां गतिः । बभाष ईषत् स्मितशोभिताननः ॥...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca iti svamāturniravadyamīpsitaṃ niśamya puṃsāṃ apavargavardhanam dhiyābhinandyātmavatāṃ satāṃ gatiḥ babhāṣa īṣat smitaśobhitānanaḥ

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having heard his mother’s laudable (lit. non-objectionable) desire which would create in men liking for the Liberation (mokṣa),[6] Kapila was rejoiced at heart. He who was the ultimate goal[7] of self-controlled saintly people[8] spoke with his face beautified by gentle smile[9].
purport:
Footnote [6]:
apa-varga-vardhanam [vardhana]—VC. and Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa treat this as a pun and explain: That which eliminates the three puruṣārthas.

Footnote [7]:
gati—The means of Liberation (mokṣa)—Bhāgavata Candrikā
The fruit; the ultimate result—Bālaprabodhini

Footnote [8]:
ātmavatāṃ satāṃ—The saintly persons who mediate on the Soul.—Bālaprabodhini
Those who realised Lord Kṛṣṇa. Self-control is their external index—Subodhinī
The devotees to whom the Self (ātmā) i.e. Kapila is the object of worship—VC.

Footnote [9]:
VC., Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa state: Kapila smiled because he was amused at the anxiety and fear of his (God’s) mother of Saṃsāra. He, however, wants to explain the Sāṅkhya-Śāstra to her for the sake of others.
Kramasandarbha.: “Mokṣa is for other people. I shall give her the highest bliss of Bhakta-hood”.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । योग आध्यात्मिकः पुंसां मतो निःश्रेयसाय मे । अत्यन्तोपरतिर्यत्र दुःखस्य च सुखस्य च ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca yoga ādhyātmikaḥ puṃsāṃ mato niḥśreyasāya me atyantoparatiryatra duḥkhasya ca sukhasya ca

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation:
The Lord said:
The yoga, which leads to the realization of the Self[10] (ātman), is for men the path to Liberation (mokṣa). Herein is the complete cessation of pain as well as pleasure.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
ādhyātnakaḥ [ādhyātnaka]: (i) Pertaining to unswerving devotion to the Self (ātman)—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, VC. (ii) Leading to the knowledge of the real ātman as distinguished from Prakṛti—Bhāgavata Candrikā (iii) The path of unswerving (fixed) concentration on the Supreme Soul, (iv) Yogas are of three types—(i) ādhidaivika useful in the realization of the Lord, (ii) ādhyātmika leading to the realization of the Supreme Soul—(iii) ādhibhautika—leading to the attainment of siddhis—Subodhinī

devanagari: तमिमं ते प्रवक्ष्यामि यं अवोचं पुरानघे । ऋषीणां श्रोतुकामानां योगं सर्वाङ्गनैपुणम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tamimaṃ te pravakṣyāmi yaṃ avocaṃ purānaghe ṛṣīṇāṃ śrotukāmānāṃ yogaṃ sarvāṅganaipuṇam
translation: Oh sinless one, I shall explain to you the Yoga complete in all respects[11] which in ancient times, I expounded to sages desirous of hearing.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
sarvāṅga-naipuṇa [naipuṇam]—efficacious by Śama, dama and other complementary observances—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: चेतः खल्वस्य बन्धाय मुक्तये चात्मनो मतम् । गुणेषु सक्तं बन्धाय रतं वा पुंसि मुक्तये ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: cetaḥ khalvasya bandhāya muktaye cātmano matam guṇeṣu saktaṃ bandhāya rataṃ vā puṃsi muktaye

translation: The mind is certainly regarded as (the cause of) bondage and liberation of the Soul[12]. When attached to the objects of senses, it causes bondage. But when attached to the Supreme Man, it leads to liberation.
purport:
Footnote [12]:
Cf. mana eva manuṣyāṇāṃ kāraṇaṃ bandha-mokṣayoḥ / Maitrī 6.34 Viṣṇu Purāṇa

devanagari: अहं ममाभिमानोत्थैः कामलोभादिभिर्मलैः । वीतं यदा मनः शुद्धं अदुःखं असुखं समम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ mamābhimānotthaiḥ kāmalobhādibhirmalaiḥ vītaṃ yadā manaḥ śuddhaṃ aduḥkhaṃ asukhaṃ samam

[nominative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śudh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]

translation: When the mind is cleansed of impurities like lust, avarice etc. born of ahaṃkāra (ego) about one’s self and one’s belongings, it becomes pure, free from (non-responsive to) pleasure and pain, and is perfectly tranquil and in balanced state.

devanagari: तदा पुरुष आत्मानं केवलं प्रकृतेः परम् । निरन्तरं स्वयंज्योतिः अणिमानं अखण्डितम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā puruṣa ātmānaṃ kevalaṃ prakṛteḥ param nirantaraṃ svayaṃjyotiḥ aṇimānaṃ akhaṇḍitam
translation: Then the man with his mind filled with knowledge, non-attachment (to the world) and devotion, realizes[13] bis Self (ātman) to be absolute, distinct from and beyond Prakṛti, immutable, self-luminous, atomic, indivisible, passive (non-participant) and (finds) Prakṛti to be powerless[14].
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Padaratnāvalī: Then the man (or jīva) realizes his Self to be the bimba of Hari.

Footnote [14]:
hatamojasam [hatamojasa]—Prakṛti which has lost its binding power.

devanagari: ज्ञानवैराग्ययुक्तेन भक्तियुक्तेन चात्मना । परिपश्यति उदासीनं प्रकृतिं च हतौजसम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānavairāgyayuktena bhaktiyuktena cātmanā paripaśyati udāsīnaṃ prakṛtiṃ ca hataujasam
translation: Then the man with his mind filled with knowledge, non-attachment (to the world) and devotion, realizes[13] bis Self (ātman) to be absolute, distinct from and beyond Prakṛti, immutable, self-luminous, atomic, indivisible, passive (non-participant) and (finds) Prakṛti to be powerless[14].
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Padaratnāvalī: Then the man (or jīva) realizes his Self to be the bimba of Hari.

Footnote [14]:
hatamojasam [hatamojasa]—Prakṛti which has lost its binding power.

devanagari: न युज्यमानया भक्त्या भगवति अखिलात्मनि । सदृशोऽस्ति शिवः पन्था योगिनां ब्रह्मसिद्धये ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: na yujyamānayā bhaktyā bhagavati akhilātmani sadṛśo
translation: In order to attain Brahman, for Yogins, there is no other auspicious way comparable to devotion directed to the Lord who is the Soul of all (as sarvāntaryāmin).

devanagari: प्रसङ्गमजरं पाशं आत्मनः कवयो विदुः । स एव साधुषु कृतो मोक्षद्वारं अपावृतम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: prasaṅgamajaraṃ pāśaṃ ātmanaḥ kavayo viduḥ sa eva sādhuṣu kṛto mokṣadvāraṃ apāvṛtam

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: The wise know that attachment (to objects of senses) is the unbreakable chain that fetters down the Soul (ātman) in the Saṃsāra. But that very attachment, if applied to the good (saintly persons) is the open gate-way to Liberation (Mokṣa).

devanagari: तितिक्षवः कारुणिकाः सुहृदः सर्वदेहिनाम् । अजातशत्रवः शान्ताः साधवः साधुभूषणाः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: titikṣavaḥ kāruṇikāḥ suhṛdaḥ sarvadehinām ajātaśatravaḥ śāntāḥ sādhavaḥ sādhubhūṣaṇāḥ

[nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: The Saints (i.e. those who follow the path described in Śāstras) are forbearing (tolerant), compassionate, friendly to all beings. They have no enemies[15]. They are quiet[16], good[17] and regard good character as their ornament[18].
purport:
Footnote [15]:
ajāta-śatru [śatravaḥ]—Padaratnāvalī dissolves this as aja+ata+śatravaḥ and explains: Who are the enemies of passions like kāma, lobha (avarice) etc.

Footnote [16]:
śāntaḥ [śānta]: (i) possessed of śama, dama—Bhāgavata CandrikāKramasandarbha.
(ii) steadfastly devoted to Nārāyaṇa—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [17]:
Sādhavaḥ [Sādhu]—(i) Who help others in achieving their objects—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) straightforward—VC.

Footnote [18]:
sādhubhūṣaṇāḥ [sādhubhūṣaṇa]: (i) To whom devotion to God is an ornament—Padaratnāvalī, (ii) Though themselves saintly, they regard other saints highly (like ornaments)—Kramasandarbha. (iii) To whom Sādhus are dear as ornaments—VC. (iv) Who regard ūrdhva-puṇḍra and other marks as ornaments—Siddhāntapradīpa

devanagari: मय्यनन्येन भावेन भक्तिं कुर्वन्ति ये दृढाम् । मत्कृते त्यक्तकर्माणः त्यक्तस्वजनबान्धवाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: mayyananyena bhāvena bhaktiṃ kurvanti ye dṛḍhām matkṛte tyaktakarmāṇaḥ tyaktasvajanabāndhavāḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: With their hearts fixed on me only, they practise firm devotion to me. For my sake, they have abandoned all other activities and their friends and relatives.

devanagari: मदाश्रयाः कथा मृष्टाः शृण्वन्ति कथयन्ति च । तपन्ति विविधास्तापा नैतान् मद्‍गतचेतसः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: madāśrayāḥ kathā mṛṣṭāḥ śṛṇvanti kathayanti ca tapanti vividhāstāpā naitān mad‍gatacetasaḥ

translation: Taking refuge in me, they listen and narrate sweet stories (about me). As their minds are fixed on me, various kinds of troubles do not affect them[19].
purport:
Footnote [19]:
Subodhinī enumerates sixteen characteristics of the saints. Of these three are bhautika (pertaining to bhūtas), four ādhyātmikas and seven ādhidaivikas. The commentary being very lengthy is not summarised here.

devanagari: ते एते साधवः साध्वि सर्वसङ्गविवर्जिताः । सङ्गस्तेष्वथ ते प्रार्थ्यः सङ्गदोषहरा हि ते ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: te ete sādhavaḥ sādhvi sarvasaṅgavivarjitāḥ saṅgasteṣvatha te prārthyaḥ saṅgadoṣaharā hi te
translation: Oh pious Lady, there are the saints who have dissociated themselves from all attachments. Hence association with such deserves to be sought by you. (You should associate yourself with them) as they remove the evil of attachment (to worldly objects).

devanagari: सतां प्रसङ्गान् मम वीर्यसंविदो । भवन्ति हृत्कर्णरसायनाः कथाः । तज्जोषणादाश्वपवर्गवर्त्मनि । श्रद्धा रतिर्भक्तिरनुक्रमिष्यति ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: satāṃ prasaṅgān mama vīryasaṃvido bhavanti hṛtkarṇarasāyanāḥ kathāḥ tajjoṣaṇādāśvapavargavartmani śraddhā ratirbhaktiranukramiṣyati

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [locative single from √ kram class 1 verb], [locative single from √ kram class 4 verb]
[locative single from √ kram class 1 verb], [locative single from √ kram class 4 verb]

translation: In association with saints, there are (discussions about my) stories which give proper and complete knowledge about my power and which are pleasant to the ears and the heart. By serving (listening to) them, there will soon develop intense faith in, love for and devotion to Hari—which is the path that leads to Liberation.

devanagari: भक्त्या पुमान्जातविराग ऐन्द्रियाद् । दृष्टश्रुतान् मद्रचनानुचिन्तया । चित्तस्य यत्तो ग्रहणे योगयुक्तो । यतिष्यते ऋजुभिर्योगमार्गैः ॥ २६...
english_devnagari: bhaktyā pumānjātavirāga aindriyād dṛṣṭaśrutān madracanānucintayā cittasya yatto grahaṇe yogayukto yatiṣyate ṛjubhiryogamārgaiḥ
translation: Due to devotion generated by contemplation of my sportive work (viz. the creation, maintenance and destruction of the universe), a man gets disgusted with the pleasures of senses and to the objects seen (in this world) and to those reported (available in the world beyond)[20]. Being alert, and by practising Yoga, he tries to control his mind by easy and straight courses of bhakti Yoga.
purport:
Footnote [20]:
dṛṣṭa-śrutāt: Seen by persons of higher (spiritual) status and heard by those belonging to the lower status—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: असेवयायं प्रकृतेर्गुणानां । ज्ञानेन वैराग्यविजृम्भितेन । योगेन मय्यर्पितया च भक्त्या । मां प्रत्यगात्मानमिहावरुन्धे ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: asevayāyaṃ prakṛterguṇānāṃ jñānena vairāgyavijṛmbhitena yogena mayyarpitayā ca bhaktyā māṃ pratyagātmānamihāvarundhe

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: By not enjoying the guṇas of Prakṛti[21] and by his knowledge reinforced by Vairāgya (non-attachment) and by Yoga and devotion concentrated on me, he attains to me, who reside in all beings, in this very human body[22].
purport:
Footnote [21]:
guṇāṇām asevayā—Not enjoying articles of food (drink etc.) prohibited by Śāstras and polluted or desecrated by the outcastes. This emphasizes proper discrimination in enjoyment—Bhāgavata Candrikā

Footnote [22]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā strongly objects to the concept of ‘Liberation—while alive’. Jīvan-mukti—This means while in meditation, a man visualizes or experiences Brahman.

devanagari: देवहूतिरुवाच । काचित् त्वय्युचिता भक्तिः कीदृशी मम गोचरा । यया पदं ते निर्वाणं अञ्जसा अन्वाश्नवै अहम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: devahūtiruvāca kācit tvayyucitā bhaktiḥ kīdṛśī mama gocarā yayā padaṃ te nirvāṇaṃ añjasā anvāśnavai aham

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Devahūti said:
What type of devotion is proper for you? Of what description is it? So that thereby I can quickly and easily attain to your place namely Liberation (Mokṣa).

devanagari: यो योगो भगवद्‍बाणो निर्वाणात्मंस्त्वयोदितः । कीदृशः कति चाङ्गानि यतस्तत्त्वावबोधनम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yo yogo bhagavad‍bāṇo nirvāṇātmaṃstvayoditaḥ kīdṛśaḥ kati cāṅgāni yatastattvāvabodhanam

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh embodiment of Liberation (nirvāṇa)[23], what is the nature of the yoga spoken of by you as reaching the Supreme Lord directly like a shaft (hitting its target)? How many parts (aṅgas or auxiliaries) has it whereby one gets the comprehension of the reality.
purport:
Footnote [23]:
nirrāṇātman—(i) unexcelled bliss incarnate—Bhāgavata Candrikā, Bālaprabodhini (ii) Embodiment of highest joy.—Padaratnāvalī VC. and Siddhāntapradīpa read: nirvāṇārthaḥ [nirvāṇārtha]—The cause or means of Liberation.

devanagari: तद् एतन्मे विजानीहि यथाहं मन्दधीर्हरे । सुखं बुद्ध्येय दुर्बोधं योषा भवदनुग्रहात् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tad etanme vijānīhi yathāhaṃ mandadhīrhare sukhaṃ buddhyeya durbodhaṃ yoṣā bhavadanugrahāt
translation: Oh Hari! As I am a woman of slow understanding, please explain this to me so that I shall easily understand this difficult yoga by your favour.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । विदित्वार्थं कपिलो मातुरित्थं । जातस्नेहो यत्र तन्वाभिजातः । तत्त्वाम्नायं यत्प्रवदन्ति साङ्ख्यं । प्रोवाच वै भक्तिवितान...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca viditvārthaṃ kapilo māturitthaṃ jātasneho yatra tanvābhijātaḥ tattvāmnāyaṃ yatpravadanti sāṅkhyaṃ provāca vai bhaktivitānayogam

translation:
Maitreya said:
Having thus understood the object of his mother, Kapila felt affection for her from whom he was born (lit. got a body for manifesting himself). He explained to her the enumeration of tattvas or Fundamental Principles which they call Sāṃkhya śāstra and also the extensive course of Bhakti-yoga (Path of devotion).

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । देवानां गुणलिङ्गानां आनुश्रविककर्मणाम् । सत्त्व एवैकमनसो वृत्तिः स्वाभाविकी तु या ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca devānāṃ guṇaliṅgānāṃ ānuśravikakarmaṇām sattva evaikamanaso vṛttiḥ svābhāvikī tu yā

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
In the case of man whose mind is one, i.e, unswerving the natural, effortless, abiding disposition (devotion) of the presiding deities of the cognitive sense organs (lit. sense organs which cognise objects)and of the conative senses (which perform the acts prescribed in the Vedas) to Hari, the embodiment of Sattva is Bhakti. This unselfish (free from worldly desires) devotion to the Venerable Lord is superior to Liberation (Mokṣa) as it makes the vesture or sheath of the Liṅga Śarīra (subtle body) dissolve just as the fire in the stomach digests the food that is swallowed (eaten).

devanagari: अनिमित्ता भागवती भक्तिः सिद्धेर्गरीयसी । जरयत्याशु या कोशं निगीर्णमनलो यथा ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: animittā bhāgavatī bhaktiḥ siddhergarīyasī jarayatyāśu yā kośaṃ nigīrṇamanalo yathā

[nominative single from √ jṝ class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ jṝ class 10 verb], [instrumental single from √ jṝ class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jṝ ], [vocative single from √ jṝ ], [instrumental single from √ jṝ ]

translation:
The Lord said:
In the case of man whose mind is one, i.e, unswerving the natural, effortless, abiding disposition (devotion) of the presiding deities of the cognitive sense organs (lit. sense organs which cognise objects)and of the conative senses (which perform the acts prescribed in the Vedas) to Hari, the embodiment of Sattva is Bhakti. This unselfish (free from worldly desires) devotion to the Venerable Lord is superior to Liberation (Mokṣa) as it makes the vesture or sheath of the Liṅga Śarīra (subtle body) dissolve just as the fire in the stomach digests the food that is swallowed (eaten).

devanagari: नैकात्मतां मे स्पृहयन्ति केचिन् । मत्पादसेवाभिरता मदीहाः । येऽन्योन्यतो भागवताः प्रसज्य । सभाजयन्ते मम पौरुषाणि ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: naikātmatāṃ me spṛhayanti kecin matpādasevābhiratā madīhāḥ ye sabhājayante mama pauruṣāṇi

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb]

translation: Some do not desire to get oneness with myself (i.e. Sāyujya Mukti).[24] They are delighted in serving (worshipping) my feet and are happily engaged in activities for my sake[25]. With deep interest and affection, the devotees of the Supreme Lord enjoy in describing to one another my exploits.
purport:
Footnote [24]:
ekātmatām: (i) The state of being similar in attributes like me—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Liberation which I confer on them—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) Oneness with Brahman—VC.

Footnote [25]:
madīhāḥ—They entertain a strong desire to enjoy my beauty, sweetness etc.—VC.

devanagari: पश्यन्ति ते मे रुचिराण्यम्ब सन्तः । प्रसन्नवक्त्रारुणलोचनानि । रूपाणि दिव्यानि वरप्रदानि । साकं वाचं स्पृहणीयां वदन्ति ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: paśyanti te me rucirāṇyamba santaḥ prasannavaktrāruṇalocanāni rūpāṇi divyāni varapradāni sākaṃ vācaṃ spṛhaṇīyāṃ vadanti

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Oh mother, these saints see my splendid heavenly forms with beautiful kindly face and reddish eyes—forms which bestow blessings on them. They speak with them in affectionate terms.

devanagari: तैर्दर्शनीयावयवैरुदार । विलासहासेक्षितवामसूक्तैः । हृतात्मनो हृतप्राणांश्च भक्तिः । अनिच्छतो मे गतिमण्वीं प्रयुङ्क्ते ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tairdarśanīyāvayavairudāra vilāsahāsekṣitavāmasūktaiḥ hṛtātmano hṛtaprāṇāṃśca bhaktiḥ anicchato me gatimaṇvīṃ prayuṅkte

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ dṛś ], [vocative dual from √ dṛś ], [accusative dual from √ dṛś ]

translation: Even though the devotees do not covet for it, Bhakti takes to the subtle state (Mokṣa)[26] the devotees, with their minds enchanted by and with their sense organs attached to those beautiful limbs of mine; my dignified movements, smiles, glances and sweet speeches.
purport:
Footnote [26]:
aṇvīṃ gatim—Commentators express their differences of opinion about this final stage called Mokṣa as per the tenets of their schools. Thus with Bhāgavata Candrikā it is ‘The subtle path called archir-mārga [arcirmārga?] leading to Mukti. It is subtle as it is traversed by few’. With Padaratnāvalī it is sāyujya-1akṣaṇā mukti wherein the jīva has similarity in attributes with God. To Kramasandarbha. and VC. it is subtle as it is beyond Prakṛti and hence difficult to grasp and giving “attendantship” (pārṣadatva) of God. To Bālaprabodhini, it is the very resplendent subtle body of the residents of Vaikuṇṭha.

devanagari: अथो विभूतिं मम मायाविनस्तां । ऐश्वर्यमष्टाङ्गमनुप्रवृत्तम् । श्रियं भागवतीं वास्पृहयन्ति भद्रां । परस्य मे तेऽश्नुवते तु लोके ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: atho vibhūtiṃ mama māyāvinastāṃ aiśvaryamaṣṭāṅgamanupravṛttam śriyaṃ bhāgavatīṃ vāspṛhayanti bhadrāṃ parasya me te

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ spṛh class 10 verb]

translation: After (the disappearance of Avidyā) they do not aspire after the magnificent enjoyments and prosperity (in Satyaloka and other places) of mine, the Lord of Māya. Nor do they wish for the eight super-human powers (siddhis) which naturally flow form Bhakti. Nor do they long for the auspicious wealth of the Supreme Lord in Vaikuṇṭha. But they do enjoy it in my region, the Vaikuṇṭha.

devanagari: न कर्हिचिन्मत्पराः शान्तरूपे । नङ्क्ष्यन्ति नो मेऽनिमिषो लेढि हेतिः । येषामहं प्रिय आत्मा सुतश्च । सखा गुरुः सुहृदो दैवमिष्टम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: na karhicinmatparāḥ śāntarūpe naṅkṣyanti no me yeṣāmahaṃ priya ātmā sutaśca sakhā guruḥ suhṛdo daivamiṣṭam

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ naś class 1 verb]
[present middle first single], [present passive first single]
[nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Mother of perfectly serene mind, in Vaikuṇṭha, my devotees are never ruined. My weapon, the Winkless Time, does not affect them as I am their beloved Soul, (and am like) a son, friend, preceptor, relative and their beloved God.

devanagari: इमं लोकं तथैव अमुं आत्मानं उभयायिनम् । आत्मानं अनु ये चेह ये रायः पशवो गृहाः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: imaṃ lokaṃ tathaiva amuṃ ātmānaṃ ubhayāyinam ātmānaṃ anu ye ceha ye rāyaḥ paśavo gṛhāḥ
translation: I take them beyond Death (i.e. saṃsāra)—those who abandon this world as well as the next and their Self which wanders in both these worlds along with their Liṅga Śarīra (subtle body) and who give up their wealth, cattle, houses and such other belongings and resort with devotion to me who am Omnipresent (or have faces on all sides).

devanagari: विसृज्य सर्वान् अन्यांश्च मामेवं विश्वतोमुखम् । भजन्ति अनन्यया भक्त्या तान्मृत्योरतिपारये ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: visṛjya sarvān anyāṃśca māmevaṃ viśvatomukham bhajanti ananyayā bhaktyā tānmṛtyoratipāraye
translation: I take them beyond Death (i.e. saṃsāra)—those who abandon this world as well as the next and their Self which wanders in both these worlds along with their Liṅga Śarīra (subtle body) and who give up their wealth, cattle, houses and such other belongings and resort with devotion to me who am Omnipresent (or have faces on all sides).

devanagari: नान्यत्र मद्‍भगवतः प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरात् । आत्मनः सर्वभूतानां भयं तीव्रं निवर्तते ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: nānyatra mad‍bhagavataḥ pradhānapuruṣeśvarāt ātmanaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ bhayaṃ tīvraṃ nivartate
translation: The terrible fear (of Saṃsāra) cannot be removed by anyone else except me, the Supreme Lord, the controller of Prakṛti and Puruṣa and the in-dwelling Soul of all beings.

devanagari: मद्‍भयाद् वाति वातोऽयं सूर्यस्तपति मद्‍भयात् । वर्षतीन्द्रो दहत्यग्निः मृत्युश्चरति मद्‍भयात् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: mad‍bhayād vāti vāto varṣatīndro dahatyagniḥ mṛtyuścarati mad‍bhayāt

translation: It is through my fear that the wind blows, the Sun shines, Indra showers rain, the fire burns and the Death does its duty.

devanagari: ज्ञानवैराग्ययुक्तेन भक्तियोगेन योगिनः । क्षेमाय पादमूलं मे प्रविशन्ति अकुतोभयम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānavairāgyayuktena bhaktiyogena yoginaḥ kṣemāya pādamūlaṃ me praviśanti akutobhayam
translation: By means of Bhakti-yoga strengthened by knowledge and renunciation, yogins resort to my feet, the (seat of) Mokṣa for eternal blessings.

devanagari: एतावान् एव लोकेऽस्मिन् पुंसां निःश्रेयसोदयः । तीव्रेण भक्तियोगेन मनो मय्यर्पितं स्थिरम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvān eva loke tīvreṇa bhaktiyogena mano mayyarpitaṃ sthiram

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛ ], [accusative single from √ ṛ ]

translation:
If mind is firmly set on me by intense Bhakti-yoga, it becomes quiescent and steady. This is the only way for attaining the highest bliss in this world.
